subject: eddy-lazzarin

sectors_excited.name: Stablecoins / onchain payments
sectors_excited.why: Stablecoins have clear, durable product-market fit — the cheapest way to move a dollar now that scaling has crushed transaction costs. Payments was always a core promise of crypto and the pieces are finally in place; I expect enterprise adoption and a wave of new, well-designed (non-LUNA) stablecoin experiments.

citations.quote: With increasingly obvious product-market fit for stablecoins, I expect more experiments here (no LUNA please).
Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together.

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge proofs / SNARKs / zkVMs
sectors_excited.why: This is my day job and the area I find most exciting technically. Decades-old theory has gone practical fast and proving keeps getting cheaper. zkVMs like Jolt — built from scratch with new techniques — will scale blockchains and unlock privacy-preserving and verifiable compute, including for AI.

citations.quote: Today we're sharing our biggest open source release yet: Jolt Alpha, our zkVM built new from the ground up.
Part of why Jolt is so fast is that we're developing it almost completely from scratch using new techniques.

sectors_excited.name: Fully on-chain games
sectors_excited.why: I've believed for years that fully on-chain games would fill the niche left by programmer-games like Zachtronics, and it's happening — Proof of Play already tops Mgas/s and EVE Frontier shows what autonomous, no-one-controls-the-server worlds can be. Games push every dimension of the stack and are a serious incentive-design domain.

citations.quote: I believe fully on-chain games will fill a similar niche in coming years.
Narrative violation: #1 chain measured by Mgas/s (the correct measure for throughput) is @ProofOfPlay — by a long shot.

sectors_excited.name: L2 scaling & blockchain infrastructure
sectors_excited.why: Cheap, high-quality blockspace is the key ingredient for the experiments that take crypto mainstream. L2 rollups plus EIP-4844 blobs have slashed fees, and light clients (Helios) and better tooling make it real. Founders choose infrastructure by present and future capacity — Ethereum can't afford to slow down.

citations.quote: Ethereum should raise the blob target to 4 and nudge the calldata cost (to make pricing smoother). I agree strongly with @VitalikButerin's proposal.
Founders are choosing their infrastructure based on present and future expected capacity. Ethereum cannot afford to slow down.

sectors_excited.name: AI × crypto (proof of personhood, machine ownership, content provenance)
sectors_excited.why: The most important pairing in tech. AI is how machines think; crypto is how machines can own — agents will need wallets, keys, and identities. And as AI collapses the cost of fake content, we need privacy-preserving proof of personhood and cryptographic provenance. These are squarely crypto problems.

citations.quote: crypto = how machines can own
How bad does this need to get before all networks need to integrate a private proof of personhood system like @worldnetwork?
Every robust method to prove the origin or history of content involves cryptography and blockchains.

sectors_excited.name: Decentralized / user-owned social networks
sectors_excited.why: Open, user-owned social is the antidote to platform gatekeeping. Farcaster is the neutral platform many of us wanted — a growing ecosystem of clients and tools that extend the network without fragmenting it.

citations.quote: Farcaster is the neutral platform we wanted all along.
The Farcaster ecosystem is growing, with already a dozen interfaces and clients, and many tools

sectors_concerned.name: Memecoins
sectors_concerned.why: Memecoins are a casino that sets the industry back — they deter great founders, hand ammunition to politicized regulators, and aren't technically interesting. Interest will come and go; the real damage is to adoption, regulation, and builder behavior.

citations.quote: Memecoins alter how the public, regulators, and entrepreneurs see crypto.
Besides undermining the long-term vision of crypto that has kept so many of us in the space, memecoins aren't very technically interesting.
When memecoins have a moment, people who know better make tortured rationalizations.

sectors_concerned.name: Token-first design / company-backed tokens posing as network tokens
sectors_concerned.why: Token-first thinking — a pie chart and a farcical 'right to vote' in place of a real protocol — is a waste of time, and increasingly a place where company-backed tokens get dressed up as decentralized network tokens. A token isn't a goal; it's an instrument for a network that can operate without a single controller.

citations.quote: A made up pie chart is not real work.
If the "tokenomics" is just a pie chart, there is no network, no potential to create value, and no point.

tokens_excited.name: ETH
tokens_excited.why: ETH is the clearest example of a network token with real fundamentals — holders get an economic interest in Ethereum, and value can accrue to the token through protocol mechanisms. My conviction is in the L2-scaled Ethereum ecosystem continuing to compound. I think in terms of networks and value-capture, not price calls.

citations.quote: ETH is a clear example of a network token. It enables holders to transact on the Ethereum Network and provides holders with an economic interest in the network.

tokens_excited.name: UNI
tokens_excited.why: Uniswap is a decentralized network whose token can capture protocol fees via a mechanism like UniStaker — the fee switch is off today, but the value-capture path is real and economically independent of any one operator. Exemplary network-token design, which is what excites me, not short-term price.

citations.quote: UNI can capture fees from the network (the switch is off right now) via a mechanism like UniStaker.

tokens_concerned.name: BTC (long-term security budget)
tokens_concerned.why: I'm constructive on BTC as the native asset of arguably the most secure and decentralized network — that gives it economic characteristics no memecoin has. But its multi-decade security budget worries me: if Bitcoin stays fully ossified, the economics that secure it may not hold. Something like a VERIFY_SNARK opcode could fix it. A long-horizon protocol concern, not a near-term price view.
tokens_concerned.age_note: security-budget concern raised 2023-07/09, not revisited in 2025

citations.quote: the security model of bitcoin needs to change if it's going to last the next few decades.

tokens_concerned.name: Pure memecoins (PEPE, SHIB, TRUMP, etc.)
tokens_concerned.why: Tokens whose only substance is the meme are zero-sum and highly susceptible to manipulation. My concern isn't that they exist — gambling will always be with us — but that they crowd out and discredit real protocol work.

citations.quote: Memecoins are tokens without intrinsic utility or value, often tied to an internet meme or community-driven movement, and not fundamentally tied to a network, company, or application.
Examples include PEPE, SHIB, and TRUMP.

risk_regime.why: I don't operate in risk-on/risk-off terms — that's the herd game. I trust the instruments (fundamentals, tech, talent) over sentiment and read price as a lagging surface over steady innovation. On fundamentals I'm as constructive as I've ever been: the tech is better, the need is clearer, and the US regulatory environment has flipped. That's a conviction to build, not a short-term market call.

citations.quote: There has never been a better time to start a project in crypto: the tech is better, the need is clearer, and the regulatory environment has changed completely. This is it.
the right balance is to be skeptical in analysis and optimistic in action

notes: I'm an engineer/builder, not a trader — I express conviction about networks, protocols, and their designs rather than token-price bets. Excitement here means I think the category creates durable value (stablecoins, ZK, on-chain games, scaling infra, AI×crypto, open social); concern means structural drag on the industry (memecoins, token-first/pie-chart design). Where I name a token it's about fundamentals and value-capture mechanisms, not price targets.
